lactose.function: Bulking agent — adds volume to tablet/capsule to make it compressible

microcrystalline cellulose.function: Excellent compressibility and binding for direct compression tablets

HPMC.function: Forms hydrophilic matrix for sustained release; also used as binder and film coat
concerns: viscosity grade must match release target

hydroxypropyl methylcellulose.function: Synonymous with HPMC — widely used matrix former for ER tablets

ethylcellulose.function: Water-insoluble polymer for extended release coating of pellets or tablets

polyvinylpyrrolidone.function: Binds particles together during wet granulation
concerns: hygroscopic — may affect stability in humid conditions

PVP.function: Short name for polyvinylpyrrolidone — common granulation binder

croscarmellose sodium.function: Rapidly breaks tablets apart on contact with water for fast dissolution

crospovidone.function: Fast tablet disintegration via wicking mechanism

magnesium stearate.function: Prevents tablets sticking to punch faces during compression

silicon dioxide.function: Improves powder flow in tablet compression and capsule filling

colloidal silicon dioxide.function: Fine-particle silica for powder flow improvement

mannitol.function: Non-hygroscopic diluent ideal for moisture-sensitive drugs; also used in lyophilization

propylene glycol.function: Co-solvent for injectable and topical formulations; humectant in gels
concerns: toxicity at high doses — especially parenteral use

polysorbate 80.function: Solubilizes poorly water-soluble drugs; used in injectables and oral liquids
concerns: can cause hypersensitivity reactions in injections

PLGA.function: Forms nanoparticles and microspheres for controlled/targeted delivery

lecithin.function: Forms liposomes and emulsions; natural phospholipid

carbomer.function: Forms viscous gels for topical and vaginal formulations
concerns: requires neutralization with NaOH or triethanolamine

sorbitol.function: Sweetener in oral solutions and syrups; non-cariogenic
concerns: osmotic laxative effect at high doses

sucrose.function: Traditional syrup base and tablet coating sugar
